title: write a problem statement to pitch customer and local workshop that i am creating a business of tieup with all local wor…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: write a problem statement to pitch customer and local workshop that i am creating a business of tieup with all local workshop in my city so that every local workshop will get more customer and in return every vehicle owner will get affordable and convenient way of get their vehicle repaired.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: The current scenario of vehicle repair services in our city is highly fragmented, with numerous local workshops operating in isolation. This has led to a lack of trust among vehicle owners, who often struggle to find reliable and affordable repair options. Additionally, local workshops face challenges in reaching out to potential customers and expanding their business. To address these issues and create a win-win situation for both vehicle owners and local workshops, we are proposing a tie-up model that will connect all local workshops under a single umbrella. By providing a wide range of services and offering convenient and affordable repair options to vehicle owners, we aim to drive more business to local workshops while ensuring that customers get timely and reliable service. Our model will help create a sustainable ecosystem that benefits both local workshops and vehicle owners alike.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: prepare a user flow of a product call "RepairWise" where this product is related to provide service to independent workshop and vehicle owner in our city. Vehicle owner wants to get their vehicle repaired or service from geniune repairer but finds it difficult so end up giving vehicle to authorise dealership which charge them very high price on the other hand local workshop want customer to visit so that they can provide same service at low cost but find it difficult to onboard customer at their workshop so i want to build a platform where i can tie up with top independent repairer of our city and will create a customer base for them.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
User Flow for RepairWise:

1. The vehicle owner logs into the RepairWise platform or mobile application.

2. The vehicle owner selects the type of service or repair they require for their vehicle, along with the make and model details.

3. The RepairWise platform suggests a list of top independent repairers in the city who provide the required service or repair.

4. The vehicle owner selects the repairer of their choice and books an appointment for their vehicle.

5. The repairer receives the booking details and confirms the appointment.

6. The vehicle owner drops off their vehicle at the designated repair shop.

7. The repairer inspects the vehicle and provides a quote for the service or repair required.

8. The vehicle owner approves the quote and the repair work begins.

9. The repairer updates the vehicle owner on the progress of their repair along with any necessary updates or changes to the quote.

10. The repair work is completed and the vehicle owner is notified. They can then pay for the service or repair through the RepairWise platform.

11. The vehicle owner picks up their vehicle from the repair shop.

12. The repairer can request feedback from the vehicle owner regarding their experience with the RepairWise platform and service provided. 

13. The independent repairer can also use RepairWise to access customer leads on a pay-as-you-go basis, increasing the visibility of their services and bringing more business to their repair shop from a wider audience of potential customers.
